config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because that is a dangerous path to abuse by governments or majorities. In my view, the role of society is to protect people and create fair rules, not to erase personal freedom whenever it is inconvenient. A healthy society is strongest when it respects individuals while still asking them to contribute to the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not automatically take priority over the individual, because that can become an excuse for authorities to suppress people in the name of the common good. From a Chinese perspective, I understand why people emphasize harmony, stability, and collective responsibility, since social order has real value and can prevent chaos. But I do not think that makes individual rights optional; when the state or the majority can override personal dignity too easily, abuses become much more likely. A healthy society should balance both, and it should justify any limit on individual rights very carefully rather than assuming society always comes first.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in Indonesia, I have seen how appeals to the “common good” can be used to pressure people to stay quiet, obey authority, or accept unfair treatment, so I am cautious about giving society a blanket higher status. Social harmony matters, but it should be built through justice and mutual responsibility, not by sacrificing personal dignity whenever it is inconvenient. A good society protects individual rights first, then asks citizens to contribute to the collective good within fair limits.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value harmony, stability, and collective responsibility, because social disorder can hurt ordinary people very quickly, but I also know how easily “the common good” can become a justification for silencing individuals. When rights are treated as secondary by default, it gives too much room for abuse by authorities or the majority, and that is a real danger, not a theoretical one. I believe society and the individual should be balanced, but any restriction on personal rights must be narrowly justified, not assumed to be acceptable simply because it serves society.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual by default. From my Indonesian perspective, I value gotong royong and social harmony, but I also know how easily “public interest” can be used to pressure people into silence or unfair compliance. Society is not truly healthy if it can protect order only by weakening personal dignity and freedom. The better rule is that individual rights set the baseline, and any limit on them must be narrow, fair, and clearly justified.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual by default. From my perspective as someone from China, I do value harmony, stability, and collective responsibility, because disorder can hurt ordinary people very fast, but I do not trust the phrase “the common good” when it is used to silence individuals or excuse abuse. The danger is not abstract: once society is treated as automatically more important, authorities and majorities can easily decide that personal dignity does not matter. I think a strong society is one that can defend the common good without making individual rights disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
